$comment: Committed deterministic snapshot of eval/baseline-selector.mjs's exact output against the live 23-tool registry + eval-tasks.json v2, captured 2026-07-19. Tier A (bag-of-words) is FULLY DETERMINISTIC — no key, no network, no model sampling — so this snapshot is exact and safe to gate against byte-for-byte. baseline-selector.mjs now gates as REGRESSION-VS-THIS-BASELINE (FIX 2, gate-hardening pass): fail if exact_accuracy drops below the value below, OR a NEW forbidden hit appears (forbidden_hits grows beyond the set below), OR a task listed here with correct:true now scores correct:false. Do NOT gate on forbidden_hits.length===0 — the 2 recorded forbidden hits (T03, T04) are expected weak-selector noise (a bag-of-words heuristic confusing novada_search with novada_research/novada_scrape), not a defect to chase to zero; see baseline-selector.mjs's BASELINE_FLOOR doc and eval-tasks.json's own task notes for T03/T04. Update this file deliberately (new date + note why) whenever eval-tasks.json or the registry's tool descriptions change on purpose — never regenerate it just to make a red run go green.
